Name: Before Pink Guy
Notes:
(??/??/2008) (George produces his first beat in GarageBand)
(12/06/2012) (George starts releasing music under Pink Guy)
Date Made:
This era contains all of Joji's music before he started making music with the
Pink Guy character. He first went by "MC George" in the video "Greatest Song Ever" before he performed with the rap group Beats N' Baby Miso, which was when he started going by "MC Rukuss". He would create his first "song" with the Filthy Frank character in the video "ASIAN NERD RAPS" and another year later he created a SoundCloud account where he published songs under "LIL D".

Name: JOJI [V1]

Date Made:
Before the release of "PINK GUY", a "JOJI" album was mentioned in the video "CHIN CHIN IS NOT PLEASED". Intended for release in the summer of 2014, said to have nearly everything that we've heard before that wasn't on "PINK GUY". However, the project of course never ended up releasing in 2014, and was it later revamped and worked on further.

Note: The cover art used is from 'medicine beat' on the pinkomega SoundCloud.

Name: In Tongues
Notes:
(10/27/2017) (Lost Instruments is released as promotion for In Tongues)
(11/03/2017) (In Tongues is released)
Date Made: After scrapping most of "Chloe Burbank Volume 1", Joji began to work on converting the project into an EP for the following months, eventually becoming "In Tongues". Some reminants of the "Chloe Burbank Volume 1" project can be found throughout the EP, as "Pills" actually interpolates "Thom" from the previous project.

Name:
Head In The Clouds
aka. 88 Degrees and Rising
Notes:
(05/07/2018) (Sean Miyashiro announces the Head In The Clouds album)
(09/22/2018) (Head In The Clouds is released)
Date Made: The first collaborative project between all of the artists that are signed to 88rising's label. Joji would work on the "Head In The Clouds" project alongside working on his first full length album under Joji. This project would influence Joji to begin working on more pop songs, which is very distinct in his songs that would follow after "BALLADS 1".

Name: BALLADS 1
Notes:
(11/03/2017) (In Tongues is released)
(10/26/2018) (BALLADS 1 is released)
Date Made: After the release of the "In Tongues" EP. Joji began to work on his next project, his first full length album under Joji, which he would work on alongside the "Head In The Clouds" project. Joji would begin working with a lot more producers and artists, and do lots of live shows while working on the project, even performing unfinished versions of songs.

Notes:
A teaser for the "BALLADS 1" album released on Joji's Instagram.

Name: Nectar
Notes:
(10/26/2018) (BALLADS 1 is released)
(09/25/2020) (Nectar is released)
Date Made: After the release of the "BALLADS 1" project, Joji would begin working with more and more producers and writers, and eventually leaving behind the lofi & trap sounds that many of us became used to over the years. There was lots of marketing in between the releases, which made the project very memorable as well. The project was announced on April 17th 2020, and was planned for release on July 10th 2020. However, on June 12th 2020, Joji announced that the album had been delayed to September 25th, 2020, citing the COVID-19 pandemic. A month after the project's release, Joji would do a paid virtual live stream titled "The Extravaganza" with live perfomances and comedic skits.

Name: SMITHEREENS
Notes:
(09/25/2020) (Nectar is released)
(11/04/2022) (SMITHEREENS is released)
Date Made: After the release of "Nectar" and after the "Nectar: The Finale" tour had concluded, there was hiatus from Joji for six months before the release of the lead single "Glimpse of Us" which leaked days prior to the song's announcement, with the "SMITHEREENS" album releasing a few months later. The project has two discs, the first disc includes songs that Joji only wrote on and sang, but the second disc starts off with "NIGHT RIDER" which switches up the flow of the project, going from melancholic ballads to a trap beat with electronic-like elements. Sadly, the project continues with more songs that Joji only wrote on and sang like "1AM FREESTYLE", with the exception of "YUKON (INTERLUDE)".

Name: Chloe Burbank Volume 1 [V2]
Notes:
(05/11/2014) (A "JOJI" album is announunced in the video "CHIN CHIN IS NOT PLEASED")
(??/??/2017) (Chloe Burbank is scrapped because of sample clearance issues and is then reworked into two seperate EPs, which later become Lost Instruments and In Tongues)
Date Made: Two singles released a year later in 2015 with an announcement for "Chloe Burbank Volume 1" on social media. Joji began releasing more tracks on SoundCloud throughout the following years before signing to 88rising in hopes of releasing the project, but he was unfortunately met with sample clearance issues that he'd complain about on Twitter, and ultimately decided the decision to scrap the project, and began converting the project into a smaller EP.

Notes:
(11/04/2022) (SMITHEREENS is released)
(??/??/2025) (Joji leaves the 88rising label and forms Palace Creek)
Date Made: Hours after the release of "SMITHEREENS", Joji announced another tour with the name "SMITHEREENS: OBLIVION" a small tour with only three dates. But before that tour even finished, Joji announced another tour with the name "PANDEMONIUM", which was finished earlier than planned due to health reasons. During this time, it is possible Joji was making another project under 88rising, but was ultimately scrapped once he left the label.
